title: Implement LLM-driven itinerary agent at muno/playbooks/itinerary-planner.yaml (trip-planner Round 4b)
description: Build the trip-planner's LLM-driven itinerary agent inside the muno repo at `muno/playbooks/itinerary-planner.yaml`. Hybrid input (scripted widget submissions + free-form chat). Per-turn LLM extraction step infers slot updates and tool requests from the latest event, given current slot state. Tool dispatch through mcp/duffel + mcp/google-places + mcp/amadeus + mcp/firestore (event sourcing). Emits widget envelopes that match the 23 schemas frozen in round 20260513-000000. Hotels source LOCKED to Amadeus per round 20260512-230000 (Duffel Stays sales-gated). Cap: 1 muno PR (the playbook + its supporting agent docs/scripts). User-action: NONE — all dependencies (mcp/firestore on GKE, schemas in muno, secrets in GCP) are already in place. Scoping reference: `sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md` sections 'Agent: hybrid linear + free-form input', 'Event-sourced storage + replay', 'Pre-templated widgets'. Runs PARALLEL with round 20260513-010500 (Round 6b — real Material widget components); the two rounds touch disjoint paths in muno.

pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.status: DONE. No new secrets, no new IAM, no new GCP services. All upstream rounds GREEN.
what_is_ready: mcp/firestore v6 on GKE (catalog 625474366091821164) — event sourcing + transactional seq + header redaction.
mcp/duffel with search + test-env orders (round 20260512-220000 GREEN, catalog v5).
mcp/amadeus (existing, with documented test 500-flake on hotels — Round 4 hotels source).
muno bootstrap at noetl/muno main (commit 4143d4c, container build GREEN). 23 widget schemas + envelope at muno/playbooks/widget-contract/. Stub renderer dispatches by widget_type.
Firestore Native db `(default)` in us-central1 with worker SA datastore.user.

design_brief_for_codex.playbook_location: `muno/playbooks/itinerary-planner.yaml` — lives in muno (not repos/ops) per the 'home base' rule. Trip-planner-specific. Loaded into NoETL via the same registration script that mcp/* playbooks use, after a small extension that scans muno/playbooks/ in addition to the ops/automation/agents/ paths. If extending the registration script is non-trivial, document the manual register step in `muno/playbooks/deployment/register-with-noetl.sh` and move on.
design_brief_for_codex.workload_shape:
```yaml
workload:
  pg_auth: pg_k8s
  gcp_auth: google_oauth
  ai_provider: openai             # openai | anthropic | vertex-ai | ollama
  llm_extraction_model: gpt-4o    # JSON-mode required
  llm_chat_model: gpt-4o-mini     # cheaper for short bot bubbles
  thread_id: ''                    # UUID, caller-provided or agent-generated on first turn
  user_uid: ''                     # 'guest' for unauthenticated
  event_type: ''                   # user_message | user_widget_submit | user_widget_cta_click
  event_payload: {}                # type-specific shape — see event schemas below
  firestore_database: '(default)'
  amadeus_env: test
  duffel_env: test
  flight_provider: duffel          # inherited from travel runtime default
  enrich_with_google_places: true  # this agent always enriches; not opt-in
```
design_brief_for_codex.per_turn_loop_pseudocode:
Each agent invocation processes exactly ONE input event and emits zero or more output events. The agent is stateless across invocations — all state lives in Firestore.

```
1. Read inputs: thread_id, event_type, event_payload.
2. Load thread state (project slot_state from /chat_threads/{thread_id}/events).
3. Append the input event: mcp/firestore.append_event(
     thread_path='chat_threads/{thread_id}',
     event={ type: event_type, payload: event_payload }
   ).
4. LLM extraction step (JSON mode):
     prompt: system + slot_schema + current_slot_state + last N events + new event
     output: { slot_updates: {...}, tool_requests: [...], render_intent: {...} }
5. If slot_updates non-empty:
     a. Apply to slot_state (in-memory).
     b. append_event(type='agent_slot_update', payload={changes: slot_updates}).
     c. Update /trips/{trip_id} projection (if trip exists yet).
6. For each tool_request in tool_requests:
     a. append_event(type='agent_tool_call', payload={tool, arguments}).
     b. Invoke the tool (mcp/duffel | mcp/google-places | mcp/amadeus | mcp/firestore).
        - REDACT Authorization + sensitive headers in the request snapshot before storage.
     c. append_event(type='agent_tool_response', payload={tool, status, response_summary}).
     d. ALSO write the full request/response to events/api_calls/{call_id} for replay.
     e. Update slot_state with any auto-computable fields (e.g., result counts).
7. LLM chat step (cheaper model):
     prompt: system + slot_state + tool_responses + render_intent
     output: { bot_message?: str, widgets: [<envelope>, ...] }
8. For each widget envelope:
     a. VALIDATE against the schema in muno/playbooks/widget-contract/. Invalid envelope → swap for a bot_text widget with a graceful description (never crash).
     b. append_event(type='agent_widget_emit', payload={envelope}).
9. If bot_message present: append_event(type='agent_chat', payload={text: bot_message}).
10. Return: { thread_id, emitted_event_seqs: [...], final_slot_state }.
```
design_brief_for_codex.slot_state_schema:
Stored as a projection at `chat_threads/{thread_id}/slot_state`. Fields:
  - `region`: { label, country_code, city_code?, kind } | null
  - `check_in_date`: ISO date | null
  - `check_out_date`: ISO date | null
  - `nights`: int (derived from dates) | null
  - `party`: { rooms, adults, children: [{age}] } | null
  - `star_rating_min`: int | null
  - `budget_min` / `budget_max`: { amount: int, currency: str } | null
  - `bed_type`: str | null
  - `amenities_required`: [str] | null
  - `flight_search_results`: [<offer_id>] | null (last search batch)
  - `picked_flight_offer_id`: str | null
  - `hotel_search_results`: [<hotel_id>] | null
  - `picked_hotel_id`: str | null
  - `total_results_seen`: int | null (for 'Rooms matching: 54' indicator)
  - `matching_with_current_filters`: int | null
  - `total_cost`: { amount, currency } | null (computed at finalize)
design_brief_for_codex.trip_projection_schema: Stored as `trips/{trip_id}` once the user has provided at least region + dates. Mirrors the scoping doc's Firestore data model. Subcollections: `events/` (calendar entries: flight_depart, flight_arrive, check_in, check_out — populated by Round 5 Google Calendar), `orders/` (Duffel order snapshots), `notes/` (user-typed text notes). `chat_thread_id` cross-links back.
design_brief_for_codex.tool_dispatch_rules:
The LLM extraction step decides which tools to call. Rules of thumb the system prompt enforces:
  - If region is empty AND user mentioned a destination: call `mcp/google-places.search_text` (broad query, no specific intent) OR `mcp/duffel.search_places` (if context is clearly a flight origin/destination).
  - If region + dates + party complete AND no flight results yet: call `mcp/duffel.search_offers`. Default `flight_provider: duffel`.
  - If user picked a flight offer AND no hotel results yet: call `mcp/amadeus.search_hotels` (hotels source LOCKED to Amadeus per Round 2 outcome).
  - If user picked both: emit `itinerary_summary` widget and offer Confirm CTA.
  - On Confirm: call `mcp/duffel.create_order` with the picked offer + traveller data + balance payment. Then emit `order_confirmation` widget.
  - If the user's free-form message conflicts with current state (e.g. 'actually make it 4-star'): apply the slot update and re-run the relevant search.
  - The system prompt MUST forbid the LLM from calling production-mode endpoints. Workload always passes `amadeus_env: test`, `duffel_env: test`. The LLM cannot override these.
design_brief_for_codex.widget_envelope_emission:
All emitted widgets match a schema in `muno/playbooks/widget-contract/*.schema.json`. The agent's LLM chat step is constrained to JSON output and is given the schema list as context. The validation step (per-turn-loop step 8.a) is a HARD gate: invalid envelope → swap for `bot_text` describing the agent's intent in natural language. Never let an unvalidated envelope reach Firestore.

Widget choice heuristics:
  - Empty slots → ask via `bot_text` + `date_range_picker` / `party_picker` / `place_autocomplete_input`.
  - Flight results → `flight_list` containing `flight_card` items.
  - Hotel results → `hotel_list` containing `hotel_card` items (incl. upsell_banner if filter relaxation would help — agent computes this from `total_results_seen` vs `matching_with_current_filters`).
  - Tool error → `error_card` with retry CTA.
  - Async tool in flight → `loading_card`.
  - Ambiguous user input → `clarify_question`.
  - End of flow → `itinerary_summary` then `order_confirmation`.
design_brief_for_codex.replay_determinism: The agent is deterministic given (events + tool responses replayed from events/api_calls). To enable this: when invoked in 'replay mode' (workload field `replay: true` + `replay_thread_id`), the agent does NOT call live tools; it reads tool responses from events/api_calls for each tool_call event and feeds them through the same LLM extraction + chat steps. LLM determinism comes from `temperature: 0` + model pinning (record the model version on each agent invocation). Note: LLMs are never truly deterministic; the goal is 'mostly-stable diff' not byte-equality. Replay surfaces drift, doesn't fail on it.
design_brief_for_codex.muno_repo_changes:
Files this round adds inside muno:
  - `muno/playbooks/itinerary-planner.yaml` — the agent itself.
  - `muno/playbooks/agent/system_prompt_extraction.md` — system prompt for the extraction step (JSON-mode contract, slot schema, tool catalog).
  - `muno/playbooks/agent/system_prompt_chat.md` — system prompt for the chat-response step (widget contract, bounded fields).
  - `muno/playbooks/agent/widget_envelope_examples.md` — one example envelope per widget type (helps the LLM emit valid shapes).
  - `muno/docs/architecture/agent-design.md` — fills out the stub from round 4a/6a with the actual per-turn loop, slot schema, tool dispatch rules, replay mode, etc.
  - `muno/scripts/itinerary_agent_smoke.sh` — happy-path smoke driver: creates a thread, sends a free-form 'trip to Miami next month for 2 adults' message, polls for completion, prints the emitted event log + final slot_state.
  - `muno/playbooks/deployment/register-with-noetl.sh` — registers itinerary-planner.yaml against the NoETL server (or document manual registration if the script is non-trivial).
  - `muno/memory/inbox/codex/2026/05/<timestamp>-itinerary-agent-4b-green.md` — outcome memory in muno per the home-base rule.

Files this round does NOT touch (Round 6b's territory):
  - `muno/src/**` (frontend)
  - `muno/src/components/widgets/*.tsx` (stubs replaced by Round 6b)
  - `muno/src/theme.ts` (Round 6b)
  - `muno/package.json` (no new runtime deps; LLM SDK lives in repos/ops or NoETL core if needed)

This disjointness is what lets 4b and 6b run truly parallel.
design_brief_for_codex.ai_provider_implementation: Reuse the existing `ai_provider: openai | anthropic | vertex-ai | ollama` switch from `repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml`. For the extraction step, require a model that supports strict JSON output mode (gpt-4o, claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022, vertex gemini-2.0-pro). For the chat step, cheaper models OK (gpt-4o-mini, claude-3-5-haiku, gemini-flash). If the worker can't reach the chosen provider: AMBER on that smoke, fall through to a recorded fixture for the parts not requiring live LLM.

1_verify_pre_handoff.objective: Confirm all upstream rounds are GREEN and muno main is at the bootstrap+fix SHA.
spec: muno main HEAD must match the latest pushed (currently `4143d4c` per round 20260513-002608). `git ls-remote git@github.com:noetl/muno.git HEAD`.
muno/playbooks/widget-contract/*.schema.json — 24 files present, validate via ajv compile.
muno/playbooks/ directory exists and is empty except for widget-contract/ — confirms this round doesn't collide with prior work.
mcp/firestore v6 registered on GKE (catalog 625474366091821164). Probe with tools/list.
mcp/duffel v5 with 7 tools — Probe.
Firestore (default) db reachable from worker pod via WI (probe with mcp/firestore.set_doc on a `_smoke/` path; cleanup after).
If any check fails: AMBER + STOP.
1_verify_pre_handoff.verify: All checks pass; muno main SHA captured in result JSON.

2_author_agent_playbook_skeleton.objective: Lay down the playbook structure: workload, keychain refs, the per-turn loop as YAML steps with stubs for the LLM + tool dispatch logic.
spec: Author `muno/playbooks/itinerary-planner.yaml` per `design_brief_for_codex.workload_shape` and `per_turn_loop_pseudocode`.
Reference existing keychain entries — no new credentials.
The playbook's `workflow.steps` mirrors the 10 numbered steps in the per-turn loop. Each step has a clear name and is independently testable.
Add comment headers in the YAML linking back to the scoping doc sections and to `muno/docs/architecture/agent-design.md`.
2_author_agent_playbook_skeleton.verify: YAML parses with `python -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(...))'`. No NoETL validation errors on dry-run if `noetl playbook validate` is available.

3_implement_extraction_step.objective: Build the LLM extraction step with JSON-mode contract.
spec: Author `muno/playbooks/agent/system_prompt_extraction.md` — explains the agent's role, the slot schema (paste the schema from `design_brief_for_codex.slot_state_schema`), the available tool catalog (with input/output shapes summarized), and the strict JSON output contract: `{slot_updates, tool_requests, render_intent}`.

The extraction step in the playbook reads: current_slot_state (from Firestore projection), last 10 events (from event log), the new event. It calls the LLM (provider selected by `workload.ai_provider`) with `temperature: 0` and `response_format: json_object`.

Validate the LLM response against an internal JSON Schema before applying. If malformed: retry once with a structured-output schema hint; if still malformed, AMBER on this single turn (don't crash the agent).
Implement slot_updates application: deep-merge into current_slot_state, write the result to `chat_threads/{thread_id}/slot_state` (single doc, not events).
3_implement_extraction_step.verify: Smoke A — feed a synthetic event ('I want to go to Miami next month for 2 adults') against an empty slot_state; expected output includes `slot_updates.region.label = 'Miami'` and one or both of `slot_updates.party.adults=2`, a `tool_request` for date_range_picker emission.

4_implement_tool_dispatch_and_event_log.objective: Wire the tool dispatcher and the event-sourcing append calls.
spec: For each tool_request from the extraction step: emit an `agent_tool_call` event via mcp/firestore.append_event, invoke the actual MCP tool (mcp/duffel | mcp/google-places | mcp/amadeus | mcp/firestore for trip projection writes), capture the response, REDACT sensitive headers from the request snapshot, emit an `agent_tool_response` event, AND write the full request+response to `events/api_calls/{call_id}` linked via `event_seq`.
Apply auto-computable slot updates from tool responses (e.g., `flight_search_results = [<offer_id>...]`, `total_results_seen`).

Tool error handling: if an MCP tool returns status=error or HTTP 5xx, append an `agent_tool_response` event with `error: true`, surface a `error_card` widget on the next chat step. Don't crash the agent.
4_implement_tool_dispatch_and_event_log.verify: Smoke B — single-turn dispatch where extraction emits a flight search tool_request. Expected events in the log: `agent_tool_call` → `agent_tool_response` (success), with full request/response in `events/api_calls/`. Verify header redaction by inspecting the response snapshot.

5_implement_widget_chat_step_with_valida.objective: Build the LLM chat step that emits validated widget envelopes.
spec: Author `muno/playbooks/agent/system_prompt_chat.md` — explains: bounded JSON output (envelope schema), the 23 widget templates available, the variant + ai_adjustments contract, the rule that unknown widget_type → must fall back to a bot_text describing the intent.
Author `muno/playbooks/agent/widget_envelope_examples.md` — one valid envelope per widget_type so the LLM has few-shot examples.
The chat step calls the LLM with slot_state + recent tool_responses + render_intent. Output: `{bot_message?, widgets: [<envelope>, ...]}`.

VALIDATE each envelope against the envelope schema PLUS the matching payload schema (use the same ajv-compiled schemas the frontend uses; copy them into the playbook's filesystem at deploy time, OR ship a small validator script alongside the playbook). Invalid envelope: replace with a bot_text widget that includes the original `widget_type` + a one-line natural-language description.
Append `agent_widget_emit` (one per valid envelope) and optionally `agent_chat` (one with bot_message).
5_implement_widget_chat_step_with_valida.verify: Smoke C — extraction emits `render_intent: ask_dates`. Chat step produces a bot_text bubble + a date_range_picker envelope. Both pass validation. Both land in the event log.

6_end_to_end_happy_path_smoke.objective: Full happy-path conversation from cold thread to itinerary_summary.
spec: `muno/scripts/itinerary_agent_smoke.sh` drives this: creates a new thread (POST initial empty event), sends a free-form 'trip to Miami next month for 2 adults' user message, polls for completion (no widget required of user — agent should be able to plan a stub itinerary autonomously with synthetic Miami dates if the user's message has them or defaults).

Expected event log shape (executions IDs captured): user_message → agent_slot_update (region+party) → agent_tool_call (google-places) → agent_tool_response → agent_widget_emit (place_list) → user_widget_submit (date_range_picker, synthetic) → agent_slot_update (dates) → agent_tool_call (mcp/duffel.search_offers) → agent_tool_response → agent_widget_emit (flight_list) → user_widget_submit (pick offer) → agent_tool_call (mcp/amadeus.search_hotels) → agent_tool_response (may be 500-flake — friendly failure path) → either agent_widget_emit (hotel_list) OR agent_widget_emit (error_card) → user_widget_submit (skip or pick) → agent_widget_emit (itinerary_summary).
If Amadeus 500-flakes, the friendly-failure render path (error_card) keeps the conversation alive — agent offers to retry hotels OR skip to summary. Either is acceptable behaviour.
Cleanup: delete the test thread + its events/api_calls after capturing execution IDs.
6_end_to_end_happy_path_smoke.verify: End-to-end smoke produces the expected event chain. Final slot_state has region, dates, party, picked_flight. Optional picked_hotel depending on Amadeus availability.

7_replay_smoke.objective: Verify replay mode reproduces the agent's behaviour given the recorded events.
spec: Take the thread from phase 6 BEFORE deletion. Run the agent in replay mode (`workload.replay: true`, `workload.replay_thread_id: <thread_id>`). Replay each user_* event in order; agent uses recorded tool responses from `events/api_calls/` instead of calling live tools.

Compare emitted events from the replay against the original. Allow LLM-drift in `agent_chat.text` content (LLMs aren't byte-deterministic even at temp=0); REQUIRE byte-identical `agent_slot_update` events and the same sequence of widget_types in `agent_widget_emit` events.
If significant drift, AMBER on this phase but don't block the round — drift is a known LLM property, not a correctness bug. Document the drift in the result JSON.
7_replay_smoke.verify: Slot updates byte-identical. Widget sequence preserved. Chat text drift tolerated.

spec: Flesh out `muno/docs/architecture/agent-design.md` with the actual implementation: per-turn loop diagram (mermaid), slot schema, tool dispatch table, replay-mode flow, error handling.
Memory entry at `muno/memory/inbox/codex/2026/05/<timestamp>-itinerary-agent-4b-green.md`. Cross-link from `ai-meta/memory/inbox/2026/05/<timestamp>-itinerary-agent-4b-green.md`.

Result file: `bridge/outbox/20260513-010000-itinerary-agent-4b.result.json` with: muno_commit_sha, agent_playbook_path, smoke_summaries {extraction, dispatch, widget_chat, end_to_end, replay}, drift_metrics, cleanup, verdict.

Open muno PR: `feat(agent): implement LLM-driven itinerary planner with hybrid input + event sourcing`. Merge to muno main once self-review passes. NO direct-to-main this time — muno has its first feature PR via the standard flow.
Append a paragraph to `ai-meta/bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md`.
ai-meta side: bump `repos/muno` submodule pointer to the merged muno commit. Local commit only; Kadyapam pushes ai-meta.
8_docs_and_close_out.verify: muno PR merged; ai-meta submodule pointer bumped locally; outcome memory written in both muno and ai-meta.

architectural_rules: No new GCP secrets, no new IAM, no new MCP servers — consume what's already deployed.
Hotels source LOCKED to Amadeus (Round 2 outcome). Don't try Duffel Stays.
Production-mode endpoints (duffel_env=live, amadeus_env=production) are FORBIDDEN — agent must always run test mode.
All emitted widgets MUST validate against the muno/playbooks/widget-contract/ schemas. Invalid → fall back to bot_text.
All event log writes go through mcp/firestore.append_event (mandatory header redaction).
LLM provider switchable via workload.ai_provider; extraction step REQUIRES JSON output mode.
Smokes scope to `chat_threads/_smoke-{nonce}` paths; cleanup at end of phase 6.
muno main lands the feature via PR (not direct-to-main like the bootstrap commit was).
ai-meta submodule pointer bump is local-only; Kadyapam pushes ai-meta.
If Round 6b is mid-flight when this round opens its muno PR: rebase against the latest muno main before merging (likely no conflicts since paths are disjoint, but verify).
result_file_instructions: Write `bridge/outbox/20260513-010000-itinerary-agent-4b.result.json`. Mirror prior round shapes. Required fields: id, status, summary, muno_pr_url, muno_merge_sha, ai_meta_submodule_pointer_bump_sha, pre_handoff_checks, agent_smokes {extraction, dispatch, widget_chat, end_to_end, replay}, replay_drift_metrics, smoke_thread_cleanup_confirmed, cleanup, verdict.
